checks.1_guard_uniq_FATAL: CLOSED (robust). Row now guard_uniq['x](g: &'x guard<'m,T>) -> &uniq 'x T, no free result region. Calling guard_uniq(g) mints &'x g; g is own so OWN-10 own-binding clause forces 'x within g's scope (never caller-supplied). Reviewer reopen: give v (&uniq 'x) to keep at outer 'o needs 'x outlives-or-equals 'o (GIVE-1->OWN-4); 'x inside 'i inside 'o so FALSE -> REJECT. Mirrors arena_get exactly; 'm correctly avoided (can outlive guard). New escapes ALL reject: box<&uniq> R1 ill-formed; confined struct field region param instantiates from 'x then R13 blocks escape; helper reborrow returns view only within guard scope (OWN-10 shrink + OWN-4-at-return); two views blocked by R6 freeze. Entry (g,uniq,view) is load-bearing for early-consume-of-g (view roots at interior, OWN-5 does not freeze g); MOVE keeps R7 backstop only under natural R8 rename (open flag 7, new machinery).
checks.2_hidden_write_MAJOR: CLOSED single-level. Capture at ROOT region &uniq 'b deref(xb); writer 'w:='b; scope row exhibits writes('b), 'b not scope-introduced so not dropped; EFF-2 both-ways satisfied, no false purity. Honest scope-local write correctly dropped (balanced). Residual: multi-level reborrow precision gap (finding 3).
checks.3_CONC0_reframe: NON-CIRCULAR + non-vacuous. Theorem conditional (IF forms discharge edges via fences THEN race-free); HB contains an edge only when discharged, so a relaxed queue's payload IS a race by definition -> not admitted. MM-4 memory-event granularity (release/acquire event identity + reads-from + cumulative visibility) gives the SPSC box-payload proof a concrete target. Only MM-1/MM-2 sit outside the per-form sync list, legitimately TCB-discharged (thread create/join HB, SCOPE-3) and on the CONC-V3-5 pin list -- no should-be-obligated edge left assumed.
checks.4_MINOR_fixes: EFF-1 sync order BREAKS FN-7 (finding 1, MAJOR); non-main rows are a clean superset (empty sync component, R10(c)/R12 normalize). Shareable confined-precedence CLOSED (CHECKED FIRST + NON-confined rows). AMD-8 syntactic marker CLOSED. io fault-injection CLOSED (subset+barrier, out-of-order). sync-set clone-count redundancy (finding 4).
checks.5_closed_FATALs_undisturbed: F-race-1 (spawn capture): CONC-V3-3 entries unchanged, CONC-V31-4 own/Eagain additive and sound, still REJECTS. F-race-2 (container clone): cq_tx still never-Shareable (unaffected by precedence change), clone &uniq re-mode intact, REJECTS twice.
checks.adoption_gate: Research-draft close only. Gated on five owner escalations (CONC-0 kernel landing incl. sync atom + MM-10 proofs; OWN-11 spawn-in-loop; AMD-5/7/8 ratification; clone-row re-mode; ~48k budget) + separate landing review; fresh full re-attack required.

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md CONC-V31-11 (lines 139-146) and open flag 5 (lines 516-519), vs kernel-spec-v0.6.md FN-7 (line 279)
findings.title: The new sync effect atom is never reconciled with FN-7's main-row cap, so every concurrent program's main violates FN-7 -- the delta's named consumers are unspellable at the program root
findings.detail: FN-7 requires fn main() -> unit to have an effect row 'at most allocates(heap), traps'. The scope statement ALWAYS exhibits sync (CONC-V3-6: plus allocates(heap) and sync (MM-7)); mtx_lock/cq_* carry sync; EFF-2's new both-ways exhibits clause (CONC-V31-11) propagates sync up every caller. So any main that transitively touches concurrency exhibits sync, which is not in {allocates(heap), traps} -> FN-7 rejects it. FN-7 is omitted from the CONC-V31-11/open-flag-5 companion-change list. Fail-closed (over-rejection, no data race, D1 intact), but the delta cannot express its named concurrent consumers at the root until fixed.
findings.minimal_fix: In the same companion landing (open flag 5), amend FN-7 to permit sync in main's row ('at most sync, allocates(heap), traps'), and add FN-7 to the CONC-V31-11 companion-change list so sync is reconciled against every existing effect-row consumer, not only R10(c)/R12.

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md guard_uniq row line 416 ('ISSUE uniq (interior) on source(g)') vs the fix prose and drop text (lines 421, 443, 567: entry '(g, uniq, view)' / 'view entry on g')
findings.title: The guard_uniq row books the interior-view entry 'on source(g)' while the fix's soundness argument books it on g; different places (mutex interior vs guard binding) with opposite AMD-5 consequences
findings.detail: For a guard holder g, source(g) is the mutex interior. An entry on source(g) is a sibling uniq issue on the interior -- AMD-5 (R15) rejects that at DECLARATION as provably-dead, and R5 would block it (overlaps g's own interior entry). The fix needs the entry ON g: only that freezes g via R6 (blocking a second guard_uniq and early consume) and is what R7's overlapped-drop check on g's unlock fires against (lines 421/567 say 'entry on g'). Under 'on source(g)' the op is dead (fail-safe); under 'on g' it works. No race either way, but the row misstates the mechanism the carve-out rests on.
findings.minimal_fix: Correct line 416 to 'ISSUE uniq on g (the receiver's own place), holder = returned view, R8-transferable' to match lines 421/443/567, and state this is the AMD-5 carve-out (a uniq loan on the guard BINDING, not a sibling issue on source(g)).

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md CONC-V31-6 (lines 262-270), 'root region ... the region the captured borrow is rooted at per OWN-10'
findings.title: 'Root region per OWN-10' is under-specified for multi-level reborrows; under the immediate-region reading a scope-local reborrow of a caller borrow re-hides the caller write -- the exact false-purity channel the MAJOR fixed
findings.detail: OWN-10 keys on the IMMEDIATE borrow region. Interpose a scope-local reborrow: scope 'p { let xb2: &uniq 'p u64 = &uniq 'p deref(xb); let s = spawn['p](writer, x: &uniq 'p deref(xb2)); }. deref(xb2) has immediate root region 'p (scope-introduced), so writer's writes('p) is dropped and the fn falsely exhibits no writes('b) though the child wrote 'b's storage via xb2->xb. The single-level fix example does not distinguish immediate vs resolved root. Soundness needs the RESOLVED root (OWN-6 resolved()), which is also what EFF-2 must do globally. Not a data race (scope joins before return, caller frozen by OWN-5) -- a false optimizer fact.
findings.minimal_fix: Pin CONC-V31-6's 'root region' to the RESOLVED (transitive) root region -- the same attribution EFF-2 uses for reborrow writes -- not OWN-10's immediate-region notion. Restate rule (b) over the resolved place and cite the reborrow-interposition program.

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md CONC-V31-9 (lines 128-131) vs the per-form obligation list (lines 179-181) and open flag 3 (line 510)
findings.title: The canonical sync set counts the two clone rows INSIDE 'the ten data cq_* rows', but the per-form list re-lists 'both clone rows' separately, making the audited completeness set ambiguous (10 or 12?)
findings.detail: CONC-V31-9 defines 'the ten data cq_* rows' as 8 data + 2 clone = 10 (clones included). The per-form list (line 180) and line 510 say 'the ten data cq_* rows and both cq_tx_clone/cq_rx_clone', re-mentioning the clones as if separate. Since this enumeration IS the trusted-declaration completeness audit for AMD-8, an ambiguous count weakens exactly the check that catches an undeclared-sync op. 'data cq_* rows' is also a slight misnomer for the clone rows.
findings.minimal_fix: Use one canonical count everywhere ('the ten cq_* sync rows: eight data + the two clone rows') and drop the redundant separate mention in lines 180 and 510.

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md open flag 7 (lines 521-526) and the FATAL-fix backstop (lines 445-449, 455)
findings.title: The borrow-held (g,uniq,view) entry is load-bearing for the move-then-early-consume case, not merely an over-rejection risk; 'can only over-reject, never race' holds only under a conservative R8-rename specification
findings.detail: Open flag 7 flags the borrow-held holder as new machinery but characterizes the risk as 'can only over-reject, never race'. That is true only if the machinery renames the holder on move (natural R8) and never silently drops the entry. Because the region tie does NOT cover early consumption of g (the view roots at the interior, so OWN-5 does not freeze g), the entry's R6 freeze is the sole guard against 'move the view, then consume g early while the moved view still aliases the interior'. A spec that removes the entry when its original holder is moved-from would reopen a race.
findings.minimal_fix: State the invariant the spec must preserve: the (g,uniq,view) entry stays live and follows the view under every R8 move (rename, never drop-on-move), removed only when the current holder is consumed or scope-ends; note it is load-bearing against early-consume-of-g, not cosmetic over-rejection.

verdict: CLOSE (research-draft close, NOT production). The last FATAL and both tracked MAJORs are genuinely repaired, and I could spell no checker-accepted data race -- D1 is upheld. (1) The guard_uniq interior-view FATAL is closed by mirroring arena_get exactly: the free result region is deleted and the view returns at 'x, the receiver's own region carried by the &'x g parameter; OWN-10's own-binding clause forces 'x within the guard's scope (never caller-supplied), so the reviewer's exact reopen REJECTS at GIVE-1/OWN-4, and every new escape I tried -- box/struct-field store (R1 ill-formed / R13 confined-escape), a helper that reborrows the guard and returns the view (OWN-10 shrink + OWN-4-at-return), and two simultaneous views (R6 freeze) -- also rejects. The (g,uniq,view) entry is a real early-consume backstop (load-bearing, under the natural R8 rename). (2) CONC-0 circularity is closed: the theorem is now conditional (IF every form discharges its edges via its fences THEN race-free), HB contains an edge only when discharged, and MM-4's memory-event-granularity restatement (release/acquire event identity + reads-from + cumulative visibility) gives the SPSC box-payload proof a concrete non-vacuous target. (3) The scope effect-row hidden_write MAJOR is closed for the single-level case (root-region attribution + forbidding scope-local-annotation captures), and the io-durability MAJOR is closed by the subset+barrier / out-of-order fault-injection reframe. The MINOR fixes (Shareable confined-precedence, AMD-8 syntactic marker, EFF-1 sync order for non-main rows) hold, and the two already-closed FATALs (spawn capture, container clone) are undisturbed. Residual findings are all fail-closed, none a data race: one MAJOR (FN-7's main-row cap is broken by the sync atom, making top-level concurrency unspellable -- a companion-landing omission within open flag 5, not a soundness hole), three MINOR precision gaps (guard row 'on source(g)' vs the fix's entry-on-g; 'root region per OWN-10' must be the resolved root to foreclose reborrow re-hiding; the sync-set clone-count redundancy), and one NOTE sharpening open flag 7's load-bearing entry. Fix the FN-7 companion gap and pin the three precision points, then RE-ATTACK the repaired text. Adoption remains gated on the five owner escalations (CONC-0 as kernel memory-model text with the sync atom + MM-10 target proofs; OWN-11 spawn-in-loop; AMD-5/AMD-7/AMD-8 ratification; clone-row re-mode; the ~48k spec-mass budget) plus a separate landing review; nothing here authorizes a production spec, checker, or compiler change.
hardest_failed: Two-function-boundary view laundering: a helper fn h['h,'k](g: &'h guard<'m,u64>) -> &uniq 'k u64 that reborrows the guard and tries to return the interior view at a FREE result region 'k so the caller books it past the unlock -- the exact 'free result region' shape that was the v3 FATAL, one function deeper. DEFEATED: inside h, guard_uniq(g) yields &uniq 'x with 'x within 'h; returning at 'k needs 'x outlives-or-equals 'k, but 'k is a distinct caller-supplied region incomparable to 'x (OWN-3 fails closed) -> h's own return REJECTS at OWN-4. The self-tied variant carries the view out only at 'h, which at h's call site is forced within the own guard's scope by OWN-10. No free region survives across the boundary.

Move-the-view-then-consume-g-early: let v = guard_uniq(g); let v2 = move v; consume g; set deref(v2) = 999; -- probe whether moving the view orphans the R6 freeze so g unlocks while v2 still aliases the interior. DEFEATED under the natural R8 rename (holder v->v2 stays live, R6 keeps g frozen, R7 overlapped-drop blocks the early consume), BUT this is the one place soundness rests on unspecified borrow-held-holder machinery (open flag 7) rather than the region tie -- surfaced as the NOTE, since a drop-on-move spec would race here.

Reborrow-interposition purity launder: capture &uniq 'p deref(xb2) where xb2 is a scope-local reborrow of caller borrow xb, to make writer's write attribute to scope-local 'p and drop from the scope effect row. Half-succeeded as a precision gap (finding 3): defeated only if 'root region' means the RESOLVED root ('b), not OWN-10's immediate region ('p); the single-level fix example does not disambiguate, so it must be pinned or the false-purity channel reopens.

SPSC box-payload race under the reduction: producer write *box; cq_send(box) vs consumer cq_recv -> box2; read *box2, against MM-4. DEFEATED: v3.1 MM-4 at memory-event granularity chains write*box ->po-> release-store ->reads-from-> acquire-load ->po-> read*box2 into HB; a relaxed publish fails to be a release event so it does NOT discharge, leaving the payload a genuine race -- the reduction is non-vacuous, exactly the v3 gap now closed.

Child locks a parent-held mutex across the MM-2 join (scope 'p { let g1 = mtx_lock(&'m m); spawn['p](locker, m: &'p m); }) to forge two live guards aliasing one interior &uniq. DEFEATED: MM-3 O1 admits one tenure per instance process-wide, so the child's acquire blocks (never a second token); CONC-V3-4 join-before-unlock turns it into a deadlock -- a liveness failure outside CAP-1, not a D1 race.
